articles.title: While the World Scrambles for Oil, China Sits on Full Tanks
articles.summary: The possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz may not prompt China to return quickly to prewar levels of oil purchases from the Persian Gulf.
articles.aiSummary: China has maintained high oil inventories despite global supply disruptions, reducing its need for immediate imports even if the Strait of Hormuz reopens. Analysts note that China's strategic reserves and diversified supply sources, including increased purchases from Russia, have cushioned it from Middle Eastern volatility. The country's slower-than-expected economic recovery has also tempered demand. While Persian Gulf suppliers hope for a rebound in Chinese purchases, Beijing appears content to leverage its stockpiles and wait for favorable pricing before ramping up imports significantly. This cautious approach reflects both economic pragmatism and long-term energy security planning.

articles.summary: Iran is eager to ensure that the US pressures Israel to stop the war in Lebanon, which has reeled from renewed attacks.
articles.aiSummary: U.S. and Iranian officials are set to hold technical talks in Switzerland on Sunday, mediated by Pakistan and Qatar, following Thursday's Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) aimed at de-escalating tensions. Iran seeks U.S. enforcement of a Lebanon ceasefire, citing Israeli attacks as violations. The Iranian delegation, led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, will press for implementation of the MoU, including lifting sanctions and reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. team, headed by Vice President JD Vance, aims to address nuclear concerns and Lebanon’s ceasefire. Meanwhile, Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz over ceasefire breaches,...

articles.title: Mideast Live Updates: New Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Set to Start in Switzerland
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance was expected to talk with Iranian negotiators. But the conflict in Lebanon threatens efforts to reach a broader peace and keep the Strait of Hormuz open.
articles.aiSummary: U.S. Vice President JD Vance is set to engage in talks with Iranian negotiators in Switzerland amid rising tensions over the conflict in Lebanon, which complicates efforts to stabilize the region and ensure the Strait of Hormuz remains open. The discussions aim to address broader peace initiatives, though ongoing hostilities threaten progress. The talks follow heightened regional instability, with both sides seeking to de-escalate while navigating complex geopolitical challenges. The outcome could impact global oil markets and regional security dynamics. (Source: *The New York Times*)

articles.title: Vance arrives in Switzerland for US-Iran talks
articles.summary: Both nations seek a durable end to their war as Iran slams Israeli attacks on Lebanon as violations of interim deal.
articles.aiSummary: U.S. Vice President JD Vance arrived in Switzerland for talks with Iranian officials to implement an interim deal aimed at ending the U.S.-Iran war, following a 60-day ceasefire extension agreed this week. The negotiations, mediated by Pakistan and hosted in Buergenstock, involve Iranian chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and focus on nuclear issues and Lebanon ceasefire violations. Tensions rose after Iran’s Revolutionary Guard closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon, though U.S. forces confirmed commercial vessels continued operations. Both sides seek progress, but disagreements persist over Lebanon’s ceasefire and nuclear terms.

articles.title: Protests in Albania against Kushner resort swell to tens of thousands
articles.summary: Albanians have gathered for the 21st day in a row to protest plans to build a resort on Albania's coast.
articles.aiSummary: Tens of thousands of protesters gathered in Albania to oppose a luxury resort development backed by Jared Kushner near a protected coastal area. Demonstrators expressed concerns about environmental damage and lack of transparency surrounding the project. The $1 billion development, planned for the Adriatic coast near Vlorë, has faced growing public backlash since its announcement. Albanian opposition leaders joined the protests, calling for greater scrutiny of foreign investments. Authorities maintained a heavy police presence as crowds marched through the capital, Tirana, marking one of the largest demonstrations in recent years against a foreign-led development project in the country.

articles.title: Iran war day 114: US, Iranian delegations in Switzerland for key talks
articles.summary: Lebanon to top the agenda as US and Iran to hold talks in Switzerland’s Burgenstock mediated by Qatar and Pakistan.
articles.aiSummary: U.S. and Iranian delegations have gathered in Switzerland for mediated talks aimed at de-escalating tensions, following a recent memorandum of understanding between the two nations. The discussions, facilitated by Qatar and Pakistan, come as Israel intensifies strikes in Lebanon, prompting Iran’s Revolutionary Guard to close the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation. Vice President JD Vance leads the U.S. delegation, while Iran’s team includes senior officials like Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. The conflict, now in its 114th day, has disrupted global oil markets, with prices surpassing $100 per barrel amid heightened regional instability.

articles.title: The Women Running Businesses Under Taliban Rules
articles.summary: With secondary education and most jobs out of reach, thousands of Afghan women have turned to entrepreneurship as the only path to make money and maintain a social life.
articles.aiSummary: Despite Taliban restrictions banning women from most jobs and education, many Afghan women have turned to entrepreneurship as their only viable income source. Operating home-based businesses in sectors like tailoring, baking, and handicrafts, they navigate severe limitations including banking restrictions and mobility constraints. Some sell goods through male relatives or online platforms to circumvent direct market access bans. While providing crucial livelihoods, these ventures remain precarious under the Taliban's tightening gender policies that continue to erase women from public economic life. The UN estimates over 100,000 women-run businesses have closed since the Taliban takeover in 2021.
